title: Context menus: Quick actions vs Admin tools (dbg119)
description:
PipeShare explorer and PipeSync planner context menus now split day-to-day operations under Quick actions from support and Create User under Admin tools.

Create User is a flyout submenu (path access, module settings); Add User on a folder or file stays a single quick action for granting access. Customer explorer menus get Add User under Access when appropriate.

Who is affected: OVH private server on deploy. SaaS when Mike applies the platform version in releases.html.
changeLog: PipeSync planner: ops (import, structure, move, Add User access) in Quick actions; support and Create User flyout in Admin tools.
PipeShare employee menu: Create User flyout moved to Admin tools section; customer Add User under Access on remote rows.
PipeSync app.js: ctx-create-user-menu and separate ctx-add-user-access quick action.
Cache bust dbg119 on portal runtime, PipeSync, mobile, and addon manifest.
